entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes the importance of expanding peaceful uses of nuclear energy to address climate change, drive economic development, and achieve the 2030 SDGs. It affirms that NPT States Parties are entitled to continuously benefit from such peaceful applications.
entries.quote: The peaceful uses of nuclear energy and technology is an important means to meet the global climate change challenge, expand the momentum of global economic development, and achieve the 2030 Sustainable Development Goals. States Parties are entitled to reaping constantly the dividends of peaceful uses of nuclear energy.

entries.position_summary: The country reaffirms that all states have an inalienable right to the peaceful use of nuclear energy and highlights its importance for development, addressing climate and energy challenges, and achieving the 2030 SDGs. It supports assisting developing countries in leveraging nuclear-related science, technology, and innovation for sustainable development.
entries.quote: 4. Peaceful Uses of Nuclear Energy Reaffirm peaceful uses of nuclear energy is an inalienable right of all countries, which has great significance for promoting social and economic development, responding to global challenges including climate change and energy shortage, and realizing the 2030 Sustainable Development Goals. Recall the actions committed in Pact for the Future, support developing countries in peacefully using science, technology and innovation to realize sustainable development.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes that peaceful nuclear energy and technology are vital for achieving the SDGs and addressing climate change. It calls on the Review Conference to strengthen support for the IAEA’s promotional role and to encourage international cooperation to maximize the benefits of nuclear applications.
entries.quote: Fourthly, the peaceful use of nuclear energy and nuclear technology is indispensable for realizing the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and addressing the challenge of climate change. The Review Conference should make greater efforts, further supporting the IAEA to play its unique role in this regard and to reinforce its promotional activities. Moreover, the Conference should further advocate and encourage international cooperation, so as to fully bring out the potential of nuclear energy and nuclear technology.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes that nonproliferation efforts must not infringe on NPT Article IV rights to peaceful nuclear energy, particularly for developing countries. It urges avoiding politicization of peaceful uses to prevent disruptions to normal international cooperation.
entries.quote: 16. Efforts to prevent the proliferation of nuclear weapons should not undermine the legitimate rights of peaceful uses of nuclear energy of States parties, especially developing countries. The international community should refrain from politicizing the peaceful uses of nuclear energy issues, so as not to disrupt or hinder regular international cooperation.

entries.position_summary: Supports the IAEA’s central role in promoting peaceful nuclear energy and calls for increased, needs-based assistance from developed countries and the IAEA to developing countries.
entries.quote: 17. IAEA should continue to play a central role in promoting the peaceful uses of nuclear energy. Developed countries and IAEA should provide more assistance to developing countries in the peaceful uses of nuclear energy based on their actual needs.

entries.position_summary: The country urges active support for IAEA technical cooperation and calls for adequate, predictable resources to meet growing technical cooperation needs.
entries.quote: Member States should actively support the IAEA’s technical cooperation activities to ensure its adequate and predictable resources,thus meeting the growing needs for technical cooperation.

entries.position_summary: China underscores that peaceful uses of nuclear energy are an inalienable right and a core pillar of the NPT, and advocates strengthened international cooperation in this area to advance the Treaty’s disarmament and non-proliferation objectives and promote 'atoms for peace and development.'
entries.quote: Remarks by Chinese Delegation on Peaceful Uses of Nuclear Energy at the First Meeting of the Preparatory Committee for the 2026 NPT Review Conference Mr. Chair, Peaceful uses of nuclear energy is one of the three pillars of the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT) and an inalienable right of the States parties by the Treaty. Promoting international cooperation in peaceful uses of nuclear energy contributes to the full realization of the objectives of the Treaty and to the process of nuclear disarmament and nuclear non-proliferation. "Atoms for peace and development" is the common aspiration of all peoples.

entries.position_summary: China underscores the importance of nuclear energy for socio-economic development, promotes research and industrial applications for peaceful purposes, and supports international cooperation to advance peaceful uses globally.
entries.quote: Mr. Chair, China attaches great importance to the role of nuclear energy in promoting socio-economic development, vigorously promotes technological research and industrial development and application of nuclear energy for peaceful purposes, and actively participates in and supports international cooperation in promoting the development and peaceful uses of nuclear energy in the world.

entries.position_summary: China emphasizes support for peaceful uses of nuclear technology by expanding technical assistance through IAEA channels and becoming the second-largest contributor to the IAEA Technical Cooperation Fund, contributing nearly $100 million to date.
entries.quote: China is actively expanding its technical assistance to other developing countries through IAEA’s technical cooperation channels and has become the second largest contributor to the Technical Cooperation Fund (TCF). So far, China has contributed an accumulated amount of nearly 100 million dollars to the TCF.

entries.position_summary: China supports expanding peaceful uses of nuclear energy, backing the IAEA’s central role and increased financial and technical assistance to developing countries to advance the 2030 Agenda. It supports multilateral export control mechanisms but opposes their politicization and use for decoupling or ideological purposes.
entries.quote: peaceful uses of nuclear energy and nuclear technology are growing. China supports the central role of the IAEA in promoting international nuclear energy cooperation and opts for increasing financial and technical assistance to developing countries in order to contribute to implementation of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development. China supports multilateral export control mechanism, but opposes the use of non-proliferation as an excuse to draw ideological lines, stretch the concept of national security, and use export control as a political tool for decoupling.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes that robust nuclear safety is essential for the development and application of nuclear power and technologies due to potential regional and global impacts. It urges all states to strictly fulfill national nuclear safety responsibilities to enable the peaceful use of nuclear energy without harming the environment or human health.
entries.quote: Nuclear safety is the lifeline of the development of nuclear power and the application of nuclear technologies, which not only has a bearing on the economic development and social stability of the countries concerned, but also may bring about serious regional and global impacts. All countries should strictly implement their national responsibilities for nuclear safety, so as to peacefully use the nuclear energy for the building of a community of life for mankind and nature, and not at the expense of the natural environment and human health.

entries.position_summary: The country urges stronger safety and security measures for peaceful nuclear facilities, including nuclear power plants. It opposes armed attacks on such facilities, calls for compliance with the Convention on Nuclear Safety and prevention of man-made accidents, and supports a proactive IAEA role in promoting safety and security.
entries.quote: 7. Efforts must be made to ensure the safety and security of nuclear facilities for peaceful uses. Discussions should focus on maintaining the safety and security of nuclear power plants, opposing armed attacks on nuclear power plants and other peaceful nuclear facilities, complying with the Convention on Nuclear Safety, resolutely avoiding man-made nuclear accidents, and supporting the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) in playing a positive role in promoting the safety and security of peaceful nuclear facilities.

entries.position_summary: The country highlights that emerging technologies, including AI, can advance the peaceful use of nuclear energy. It urges the IAEA and international community to enhance research cooperation in this area and ensure equitable access to nuclear energy for all states.
entries.quote: The rapid development of emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence has significant implications for promoting the peaceful use of nuclear energy. The IAEA and the international community are encouraged to strengthen research cooperation in this field and promote equitable access to nuclear energy for all countries.

entries.position_summary: The country asserts that peaceful nuclear activities must not endanger the environment or human health and calls on Japan to halt the planned ocean discharge of Fukushima’s contaminated water. It urges Japan to meet its moral and legal obligations, engage sincerely with neighboring states, and ensure safe disposal under strict international monitoring.
entries.quote: The peaceful uses of nuclear energy should not be achieved at the cost of natural environment and human health. Japan should respond to the concerns of the international community on the decision of discharging the nuclear contaminated water of the Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant into the ocean, fulfill its due moral responsibilities and international legal obligations, stop forcing through its plan of discharge the nuclear contaminated water into the ocean, and communicate with its neighbouring countries in a sincere attitude, and ensure that the nuclear-contaminated water is safely disposed under strict international monitoring.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes balancing non-proliferation with the Article IV right to peaceful nuclear energy, especially for developing states. It opposes using national security justifications to restrict cooperation and technology transfer, calling for broader access to nuclear energy and technology.
entries.quote: Second, it’s necessary to keep the balance between non-proliferation of nuclear weapons and peaceful uses of nuclear energy. Efforts to prevent nuclear proliferation should not undermine the legitimate right of countries, especially developing countries, to peaceful uses of nuclear energy. We should reject using national security as an excuse by some countries to wantonly suppress, interfere with and restrict other countries’ cooperation in peaceful uses of nuclear energy, and ensure that nuclear energy and nuclear technology will benefit more countries.

entries.position_summary: China emphasizes strong national and international efforts on nuclear safety and security, asserting compliance with related obligations and active participation in global mechanisms. It supports converting HEU to LEU for micro‑reactors and assisting countries to minimize HEU use, tailored to their needs and capacities.
entries.quote: China always gives priority to nuclear safety and security and continuously improves its national capabilities in nuclear safety and security with a good track record. At the same time, China faithfully fulfills its international obligations on nuclear safety and security, vigorously promotes international cooperation in nuclear safety and security, and deeply participates in international nuclear safety and security mechanisms and processes. China will continue to participate in cooperation on conversion of HEU to LEU for micro-reactors and support countries in minimizing the use of HEU and reducing nuclear safety and security risks, in light of their own needs, as well as their economic and technological capabilities.

entries.position_summary: Supports the Atom for Global South initiative and affirms the IAEA’s central role in promoting peaceful nuclear applications. Calls on developed countries and institutions to expand assistance—funding, technology, and personnel—to meet Global South needs, and urges sufficient, reliable, and predictable IAEA technical cooperation resources. Encourages increased voluntary contributions to IAEA technical cooperation activities.
entries.quote: NPT/CONF.2026/PC.III/WP.39 Support the initiative “Atom for Global South” and the continued central role of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) in promoting the uses of nuclear energy for peaceful purposes. Developed countries and institutions should, based on the actual needs of Global South nations, further increase assistance in uses of nuclear energy for peaceful purposes, including funding, technology and personnel. The IAEA should ensure that technical cooperation resources are sufficient, reliable and predictable, meet the growing needs of States parties for technical cooperation while giving priority to Global South nations. Encourage capable countries to continue providing and increasing voluntary contributions for technical cooperation activities aimed at uses of nuclear energy for peaceful purposes.

entries.position_summary: The country commits to sharing the benefits of nuclear energy development to support socio-economic prosperity, signaling support for cooperation and access in peaceful nuclear applications.
entries.quote: we will actively contribute to sharing the dividends of nuclear energy development and promoting socio-economic prosperity.

entries.position_summary: Supports strengthened international cooperation on peaceful uses of nuclear technology, urging implementation of UNGA Resolution A/RES/79/80. Calls for concrete measures to fulfill peaceful-use obligations, continued dialogue, and development of guiding principles.
entries.quote: Call on States parties to jointly implement the “Promoting International Cooperation on the Peaceful Uses in the Context of International Security” Resolution (A/RES/79/80) adopted at the 79th session of the UN General Assembly, take concrete measures to fulfill obligations and commitments regarding peaceful uses, continue dialogue on promoting peaceful utilization and related international cooperation, and explore the formulation of guiding principles. 5/6

entries.position_summary: China emphasizes protecting developing countries’ right to peaceful nuclear energy and urges robust international cooperation through the IAEA. It calls for full, timely, adequate, and predictable contributions to the IAEA Technical Cooperation Fund, prioritizing support and assistance to developing countries based on their needs.
entries.quote: Mr. Chair, The Global Development Initiative and the Global Security Initiative proposed by President Xi Jinping provide a solid basis for China to participate in and promote international cooperation in peaceful uses of nuclear energy. In order to give full play to the role of NPT in serving world peace and development in the new era, the international community should make efforts in the following three aspects: First, the right of developing countries to peaceful uses of nuclear energy should be preserved. All member States should actively support technical cooperation activities of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), make full and timely contribution to the Technical Cooperation Fund (TCF), ensure adequate and predictable TC resources, and give preference to developing countries. IAEA member States, especially developed countries, should increase their assistance and support to developing countries based on the latter’s actual 1

entries.position_summary: The country advocates stronger international cooperation on peaceful nuclear energy, highlighting the central role of the IAEA and calling on advanced states to support capacity building, including initiatives like 'Atoms for the Global South.' It also hopes the current NPT review cycle will generate new, creative measures to advance the treaty’s third pillar.
entries.quote: On peaceful uses of nuclear energy. With the emergence of new nuclear energy technologies and with growing number of countries operating or planning to operate nuclear power plants, international cooperation on peaceful uses should be further strengthened. It’s essential to give full play to the important role of the IAEA in this respect. States with advanced capabilities need to help capacity building of countries that want to develop peaceful nuclear energy. “Atoms for the Global South” deserves increased support. We hope this review cycle will come up with some new and creative ideas in promoting the third pillar of the NPT.

entries.position_summary: The country advocates a diplomatic resolution of the Iranian nuclear issue, opposing unilateral force and sanctions while supporting continued dialogue and negotiations. It backs IAEA–Iran cooperation to resolve outstanding safeguards concerns and emphasizes balancing rights and obligations, including Iran’s right to peaceful nuclear activities under appropriate safeguards.
entries.quote: Stay committed to peaceful settlement of the Iranian nuclear issue through political and diplomatic means, and oppose unilateral actions including the use of force and illegal sanctions that escalate the situation. Stay committed to the momentum of dialogue and negotiations, and support the IAEA and Iran in resolving outstanding safeguards issues through dialogue and cooperation. Stay committed to balancing rights and responsibilities, 4/6

entries.position_summary: Urges all States Parties to join and comply with nuclear safety and security conventions, and encourages those developing advanced and small modular reactors (including portable reactors) to work closely with the IAEA to ensure their safe and secure deployment. Emphasizes responsible, safeguarded expansion of peaceful nuclear applications.
entries.quote: Call on all States Parties to join and abide by conventions and instruments in the fields of nuclear safety and security. State parties developing advanced reactors and small modular reactors including portable reactors are encouraged to enhance cooperation with the IAEA to support the safe and secure deployment of such reactors.

entries.position_summary: The country links safe and secure nuclear operations to sustainable peaceful uses, calling for a fair, cooperative international nuclear security system. It urges all states to fulfill national responsibilities for nuclear safety and adopt a balanced, coordinated approach to nuclear security to protect human health and the environment.
entries.quote: Nuclear safety and security are the lifeline for sustainable peaceful uses of nuclear energy, thus the international community should work together to adopt a rational, coordinated and balanced approach to nuclear security, jointly create an international nuclear security system that is fair, cooperative, and mutually beneficial, and build a community with a shared future for nuclear security. All countries should strictly fulfil their national responsibilities for nuclear safety, so that peaceful uses of nuclear energy contributes to creating a community of life for human and nature, rather than sacrifice the natural environment and human health.

entries.position_summary: The country notes that despite broad convergence on risk reduction and on linking peaceful uses to the Sustainable Development Goals, the NPT Preparatory Committee has failed to reach consensus on substantive recommendations. This expresses concern about the effectiveness of the review process and implies a need to improve it.
entries.quote: Even when there is a strong convergence of interest on certain topics, such as risk reduction or linking peaceful uses to Sustainable Development Goals, the Preparatory Committee has never been able to reach consensus on substantive recommendations on those or any other topics.

entries.position_summary: The country calls for fairness, objectivity, and transparency, opposes the politicization of export controls through expanded national security claims, and defends the right of non‑nuclear‑weapon States to peaceful nuclear energy.
entries.quote: principle of fairness, objectivity and transparency. At the same time, we should firmly oppose the attempt of certain countries to stretch the national security concept, take export control as tools for geopolitical game, and undermine the right of non-nuclear-weapon States for peaceful uses of nuclear energy.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes that peaceful nuclear energy must not compromise environmental protection or human health and urges Japan to handle the Fukushima wastewater discharge responsibly. It calls for an independent, effective, long-term international monitoring mechanism with substantive participation from neighboring countries and other stakeholders to prevent irreversible consequences.
entries.quote: Peaceful uses of nuclear energy should not sacrifice the natural environment and human health. The Japanese government should properly handle the issue of Fukushima nuclear- contaminated water discharge with responsible and constructive attitude,and cooperate to create an independent, effective and long-term international monitoring arrangement with substantive participation of neighboring countries and other stakeholders,so as to avoid irrevocable consequences from the discharge. 3/3

entries.position_summary: The country argues that Japan’s discharge of contaminated water violates international obligations to protect the marine environment, including under UNCLOS and the London Convention, by risking transboundary pollution and lacking adequate transparency, consultation, impact assessment, monitoring, and cooperation. It frames the issue as a failure to ensure safe management of nuclear-related waste associated with peaceful nuclear activities.
entries.quote: 5. Japan fails to fulfill its due international obligations. According to general international law and the provisions of UNCLOS(United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea), Japan has the obligation to protect and preserve the marine environment. When disposing of the nuclear contaminated water, Japan shall take all measures necessary to ensure that activities under its jurisdiction or control are so conducted as not to cause damage by pollution to other States and their environment, and that pollution caused does not spread beyond the areas where Japan exercises sovereign rights. Japan also has the obligation to take all possible steps to prevent environmental pollution, to inform and fully consult with countries that may be affected, to assess and monitor the environmental impact, to ensure information transparency, and to carry out international cooperation. The Convention on the Prevention of Marine Pollution by Dumping of Wastes and Other Matter 1972 (the London Convention) prohibits the dumping of radioactive wastes into the sea by means of man-made structures at sea. Japan's discharging of nuclear contaminated water into the sea by means of submarine pipelines is in violation of the relevant provisions of the London Convention.

entries.position_summary: China urges inclusive international cooperation on peaceful nuclear energy with the IAEA in a central role and equitable participation by the Global South. It opposes the politicization of export controls that restrict nuclear energy cooperation and plans to advance a UNGA resolution promoting such cooperation. China also emphasizes that peaceful uses must not compromise environmental protection or human health.
entries.quote: Fifthly, ensure shared benefits and inclusiveness, and promote the cause of peaceful uses of nuclear energy. China advocates the establishment of an inclusive market environment, giving full play to the central role of IAEA, and ensuring the “Global South” participate in international nuclear energy cooperation on an equal and orderly basis. We are firmly opposed to attempts to overstretch the concept of national security, use export control as a political tool for “decoupling” and impose unnecessary restrictions on international nuclear energy cooperation. China will submit again the resolution of Promoting International Cooperation on Peaceful Uses in the Context of International Security to the upcoming session of the UN General Assembly, and we look forward to your support. In the meantime, the peaceful uses of nuclear energy should not be achieved at the cost of natural environment and human health.

entries.position_summary: China opposes Japan’s discharge of Fukushima nuclear-contaminated water into the ocean and calls for Japan to cooperate in creating long-term, independent, and effective international monitoring. It emphasizes inclusion of neighboring countries and other stakeholders in these arrangements.
entries.quote: China opposes discharging the nuclear contaminated water of the Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant into the ocean, and urges Japan to cooperate in establishing long-term, independent and effective international monitoring arrangements that involve the substantive participation of its neighbors and other stakeholders.

entries.position_summary: The country advocates a holistic approach that balances nonproliferation with peaceful uses of nuclear energy. It supports resolving the Iran nuclear issue through a step-by-step, reciprocal process grounded in the JCPOA, emphasizing mutual respect, dialogue, and negotiations.
entries.quote: NPT/CONF.2026/PC.III/WP.39 and take a holistic approach to the goals of nuclear nonproliferation and peaceful uses of nuclear energy. Stay committed to a step-by-step and reciprocal approach, uphold the principle of mutual respect, take the framework of the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA) as the basis, and reach a solution that accommodates the legitimate concerns of all parties through dialogue and negotiations.

entries.position_summary: China asserts that peaceful uses of nuclear energy are an inalienable right of NPT States Parties and highlights its commitment to international cooperation. It pledges to share technology and experience to promote peaceful nuclear energy worldwide in line with the Global Development Initiative.
entries.quote: NPT /CONF.2026/PC.I/WP.32 Preparatory Committee for the 2026 Review Conference of the Parties to the Treaty on the 2 August 2023 Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons Original: Chinese ADVANCE UNEDITED VERSION First session Vienna, 31 July - 11 August 2023 Disposal of Nuclear Contaminated Water of Japan's Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Station Working paper submitted by China Peaceful uses of nuclear energy is the inalienable right of the States parties to the NPT. For advancing the implementation of the Global Development Initiative (GDI) put forward by Chinese President Xi Jinping, China attaches great importance to international cooperation in the peaceful uses of nuclear energy, and shares its technology and experience with other countries in order to contribute to the promotion of nuclear energy for the benefit of the people of all countries and the sustainable development of nuclear energy.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes that nonproliferation efforts must not restrict states’ legitimate right to peaceful nuclear energy, particularly for the Global South. It opposes politicization and any NPT-inconsistent restrictions on cooperation, and criticizes the use of export controls as tools for decoupling or supply-chain disruption.
entries.quote: Effort to prevent the proliferation of nuclear weapons should not undermine the legitimate rights of all countries, especially Global South countries, to the peaceful uses of nuclear energy. Firmly oppose the politicization of peaceful uses of nuclear energy and the imposition of any inappropriate restrictions inconsistent with the NPT, which may disrupt and restrict normal international cooperation. Oppose any country using the excuse of non- proliferation to draw ideological lines, overstretch the concept of national security, or use export controls as a tool for decoupling and severing supply chains.

entries.position_summary: The country opposes Japan’s planned ocean discharge of Fukushima wastewater, urging Japan to halt the move, conduct broad stakeholder consultations, and ensure handling is transparent, scientific, safe, and subject to strict international oversight.
entries.quote: the disposal. Japan should face up to the legitimate and reasonable concerns of its own people and the international community, stop forcing through its plan to discharge Fukushima contaminated water into the sea, fully consult with all stakeholders, dispose of the contaminated water in a truly open, transparent, scientific and safe manner and accept strict international supervision.

entries.position_summary: The country voices strong concern over Japan’s planned ocean discharge of Fukushima nuclear-contaminated water, asserting it is unprecedented and lacks internationally recognized disposal standards. It calls on the international community to urge Japan to manage the water responsibly to protect marine environments and public health.
entries.quote: The disposal of nuclear contaminated water from Fukushima concerning the global marine environment and public health. There is no precedent of artificially discharging nuclear contaminated water into the ocean and no internationally recognized disposal standards. The international community should attach great importance to Japan’s ocean discharge of the nuclear contaminated water and urge Japan to dispose of the nuclear contaminated water in a responsible manner.

entries.position_summary: The country argues that Japan has not demonstrated the safety of releasing Fukushima’s contaminated water, noting the presence of numerous radionuclides, limited treatment options, and potential spread of long-lived nuclides via ocean currents. It warns of uncertain risks to marine ecosystems and public health in neighboring states.
entries.quote: 4. Japan fails to prove that the discharge of nuclear contaminated water into the sea is not harmful to marine environment and public health. The Fukushima nuclear contaminated water contains more than 60 radionuclides, many of which do not yet have effective treatment technologies, and some of the long-lived nuclides may spread with ocean currents, bring uncertain impact on the ecological balance of the sea areas of the neighboring countries and on the marine environment.

entries.position_summary: China argues Japan’s monitoring of Fukushima wastewater is inadequate and calls for an IAEA-led, independent, long-term international monitoring mechanism involving third-party labs from neighboring countries. It insists Japan delay any discharge until such a mechanism is in place, ensure transparency through prompt data sharing, and halt discharges if anomalies are detected.
entries.quote: 6. Japan fails to demonstrate the perfection of the monitoring program. Japan's current arrangements for monitoring the discharge of nuclear contaminated water are not sufficient enough to judge whether the discharge is qualified at the first time, which may result in the discharge of substandard nuclear contaminated water into the sea. China maintains that the IAEA should take the lead in establishing an independent and effective long-term international monitoring mechanism with the full participation of third-party laboratories of Japan’s neighboring countries as soon as possible, and Japan must fully cooperate with the international mechanism for long-term monitoring and the following review and assessment, and continue to carry out the long-term reliability monitoring of the ALPS, the monitoring of nuclear contaminated water and environments, and the assessment of the radioactive impact of environments, and disclose credible data and information to concerning parties like neighbouring countries in a prompt and transparent manner. Japan must not start discharging until the long-term monitoring mechanism is established, and must stop discharging water once anomalies are detected in the data on the discharge of nuclear contaminated water.

entries.position_summary: The country advocates designating UNODA as the permanent secretariat for the NPT review process and formalizing a supporting role for the IAEA, particularly on non-proliferation and peaceful uses. This aims to institutionalize and streamline the review process and leverage IAEA expertise.
entries.quote: • Formalizing the United Nations role by establishing the Office for Disarmament Affairs as the permanent secretariat for the Non-Proliferation Treaty review process rather than deciding separately for each Non-Proliferation Treaty review cycle. Such a decision could also formalize the supporting role of the International Atomic Energy Agency in staffing the secretariat for issues of non-proliferation and the peaceful uses of nuclear technology.

entries.position_summary: China emphasizes protecting NNWS rights to peaceful nuclear energy, supports the IAEA’s central role, and backs efforts to ensure safety and security of peaceful facilities. It opposes unilateral export restrictions and broader decoupling, and condemns armed attacks on peaceful nuclear facilities.
entries.quote: Fourthly, remain faithful to NPT's initial mission, contribute to peace and development. The rights of peaceful uses of nuclear energy of non-nuclear-weapon States should be effectively protected. As a major country in nuclear science and technology, China supports the “Atoms for Global South Initiative" and supports the central role of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA). We resolutely oppose any misuse of unilateral export restrictions that impedes nuclear energy global collaborative efforts. Nuclear technology should serve as wealth for shared and win-win benefits rather than a tool to weave an iron curtain. Decoupling will ultimately lead to self-isolation. China opposes armed attacks against peaceful nuclear facilities, supports the IAEA in playing a constructive role in promoting the safety and security of peaceful nuclear facilities.

entries.position_summary: China emphasizes protecting NNWS rights to peaceful nuclear energy, supports the IAEA’s central role and the Atoms for Global South initiative, and opposes unilateral export restrictions and decoupling that hinder cooperation. It also opposes armed attacks on peaceful nuclear facilities and backs the IAEA’s role in ensuring their safety and security.
entries.quote: Fourthly, remain faithful to NPT's initial mission, contribute to peace and development. The rights of peaceful uses of nuclear energy of non-nuclear-weapon States should be effectively protected. As a major country in nuclear science and technology, China supports the “Atoms for Global South Initiative" and supports the central role of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA). We resolutely oppose any misuse of unilateral export restrictions that impedes nuclear energy global collaborative efforts. Nuclear technology should serve as wealth for shared and win-win benefits rather than a tool to weave an iron curtain. Decoupling will ultimately lead to self-isolation. China opposes armed attacks against peaceful nuclear facilities, supports the IAEA in playing a constructive role in promoting the safety and security of peaceful nuclear facilities.

entries.position_summary: The country argues that the IAEA’s limited mandate means it did not confirm the legality, long-term safety, or data accuracy of Japan’s plan to discharge treated water, so Japan should not treat the IAEA report as authorization to proceed. It questions the sufficiency of oversight and safety assurances for the planned ocean release.
entries.quote: It must be pointed out that due to its limited mandate, the IAEA did not review the legitimacy of Japan’s discharge plan, nor evaluate the long-term effectiveness of Japan's purification equipment, neither confirm the authenticity and accuracy of Japan’s nuclear-contaminated water data. Japan should not take the report as a “licence” to start its ocean discharge.

entries.position_summary: China emphasizes the safe, orderly, and large-scale expansion of nuclear power as a key component of its clean energy strategy to meet climate goals, highlighting rapid growth and leadership in Generation-III reactors. It frames nuclear power development as both peaceful and safety-focused.
entries.quote: China pursues active, safe and orderly development of nuclear power, and regards nuclear power as an important block in building up a clean and efficient energy system to address the challenges of climate change and achieve the goals of carbon peaking and carbon neutrality. China is currently the country with the largest scale of nuclear power capacity under construction and the fastest in developing nuclear power. China is also the first country to start mass commercial operation of generation-3 nuclear power units and has played an important role in nuclear power renaissance in the world.

entries.position_summary: China emphasizes promoting the peaceful uses of nuclear energy, sharing its experience with newcomer states, and has concluded intergovernmental cooperation agreements with over 30 countries, all framed by nonproliferation principles and mutual benefit.
entries.quote: On the basis of nuclear nonproliferation, China attaches importance to sharing its experience with countries who intent to embark on nuclear energy, and has signed intergovernmental cooperation agreements on peaceful uses of nuclear energy with more than 30 countries. Through mutually beneficial and pragmatic cooperation, 3

entries.position_summary: Supports voluntary minimization of civilian HEU use and stockpiles where feasible, indicating a preference for converting to lower-enriched alternatives to reduce proliferation and security risks while maintaining peaceful nuclear applications.
entries.quote: Encourage relevant State Parties, where technically and economically feasible, to voluntarily minimize the civilian use and stockpiling of highly enriched uranium.

entries.position_summary: The country supports promoting international cooperation on peaceful uses of science and technology, including nuclear technology. It advocates balancing non-proliferation and security concerns with the right to peaceful development, while addressing security challenges arising from rapid scientific and technological advances.
entries.quote: At the 76th session of the UN General Assembly, 26 countries, including China, proposed a resolution on “Promoting International Cooperation on Peaceful Uses in the Context of International Security”, advocating the balance between development and security as well as between non-proliferation and peaceful uses, which is a full representation of the underlying logic of the Global Development Initiative and the Global Security Initiative, with a view to protecting the legitimate rights and interests of all countries in peaceful uses of science and technology, including nuclear technology, and properly meet security challenges brought about by scientific and technological development.

entries.position_summary: The statement criticizes Japan for using the IAEA’s assessment as justification for discharging Fukushima wastewater, arguing the review was requested post hoc and had a narrow mandate. It contends the IAEA did not assess the plan’s legitimacy or long-term reliability of purification systems, so its conclusions are limited and do not allay international concerns.
entries.quote: 8. Japan should not make use of IAEA’s comprehensive assessment report on the disposal of Fukushima nuclear contaminated water as “shield” or “greenlight” for the ocean discharge plan. The Government of Japan requested the Agency to conduct the review only after it had unilaterally made its decision on ocean discharge. The Agency's Technical Group was only authorized to review the ocean discharge plan, and did not examine the legitimacy of the Japan’s discharge plan or assess the effectiveness and long-term reliability of the purification devices, thus its conclusions were limited, and could not address the concerns of the international community.

entries.position_summary: The country calls for balanced advancement of all three NPT pillars, emphasizes maintaining the NPT’s authority and universality, and urges all non-parties to join as non-nuclear-weapon states. It supports eliminating the risk of nuclear war and sustained efforts toward a world free of nuclear weapons.
entries.quote: Advance the three pillars of nuclear disarmament, nuclear non-proliferation and peaceful uses of nuclear energy in a balanced way, maintain the authority, effectiveness and universality of the NPT, consolidate the international non-proliferation regime with the NPT as its cornerstone, eliminate the risk of nuclear war, make unremitting efforts to achieve a world free of nuclear weapons. States that have not yet joined the NPT should do so as non-nuclear-weapon States as soon as possible.

entries.position_summary: China urges a balanced focus on disarmament, non-proliferation, and peaceful uses to address urgent arms control challenges and produce substantive results in the current review cycle. It emphasizes upholding the NPT’s authority and effectiveness and views the success of this review cycle as crucial.
entries.quote: NPT /CONF.2026/PC.II/WP.35 Preparatory Committee for the 2026 Review Conference of the Parties to the Treaty on the 12 July 2024 Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons Original: Chinese ADVANCE UNEDITED VERSION Second session Geneva, 22 July–2 August 2024 Nuclear arms control Working paper submitted by China Today, the global security environment has experienced profound and complex changes. The international arms control regime is faced with serious challenges. Since 2010, two consecutive NPT review cycles ended without a consensus outcome. Thus the success of the current review cycle is of great importance. China believes that all sides should focus on urgent issues of the nuclear arms control area, promote nuclear disarmament, nuclear non-proliferation, and peaceful uses of nuclear energy in a comprehensive and balanced manner on the basis of mutual respect, ensure that this review cycle could produce substantive results and to jointly uphold the authority and effectiveness of the NPT.

entries.position_summary: The country underscores the NPT as the cornerstone of the regime, calling for balanced advancement of disarmament, non-proliferation, and peaceful uses, and for a successful start to the 11th NPT review cycle while upholding multilateral arms control forums. It opposes geopolitical ‘small blocs’ and nuclear cooperation it views as contrary to the NPT’s object and purpose—implicitly criticizing arrangements such as nuclear-powered submarine cooperation—while supporting peaceful nuclear uses under the Treaty.
entries.quote: Fourthly, maintain and strengthen the international nuclear disarmament and nuclear non-proliferation regime with NPT as its cornerstone. The international community should advance the three pillars of nuclear disarmament, nuclear non-proliferation, and peaceful uses of nuclear energy in a balanced manner, oppose double standards and pragmatism, firmly safeguard the effectiveness and authority of the treaty, and promote the successful start of the eleventh round of the NPT review process. We should resist all kinds of small blocs and small circles, maintain multilateral arms control mechanisms such as the First Committee of the UN General Assembly and the Conference on Disarmament, and give full play to their roles. We should resolutely oppose relevant countries’ cooperation in the nuclear field that runs counter to the object and purpose of the NPT, which are based on narrow geopolitical considerations.

entries.position_summary: The country urges prioritizing development by promoting the peaceful uses of nuclear energy, noting strong global growth in nuclear power and emphasizing the particular needs of developing countries.
entries.quote: Fourth, give priority to development and promote the peaceful uses of nuclear energy. At present, the global nuclear power development is gaining robust momentum, and the need of international community, especially developing countries, for ─ 11 ─

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes a balanced approach that safeguards the right of all states to peaceful nuclear use and international cooperation, while preventing proliferation. It cautions that nonproliferation measures should not unduly restrict legitimate access to nuclear materials, equipment, and technologies, and insists that peaceful-use claims must not mask proliferation activities. It also calls for addressing supply-demand imbalances in nuclear materials.
entries.quote: Adhere to the principles of justice and balance, and properly handle the relationship between nuclear non-proliferation and the peaceful uses of the nuclear energy. While bearing in mind the need to prevent the proliferation of nuclear weapons, no measures to prevent nuclear proliferation shall impede the legitimate rights of all States to the peaceful uses of nuclear energy and international cooperation in nuclear-related materials, equipment and technologies for peaceful purposes. At the same time, no proliferation activities shall be carried out under the pretext of the peaceful uses of nuclear energy. Attention should be paid to the imbalance between supply and demand of nuclear materials in certain countries, and effective measures should be taken to correct it.

entries.position_summary: China underscores the NPT as the cornerstone of the regime and commits to constructive engagement in the second PrepCom. It calls for balanced progress across disarmament, non-proliferation, and peaceful uses, emphasizing the NPT’s role in security and development.
entries.quote: The treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT) is the cornerstone of the international nuclear non-proliferation regime, and an important pillar of the global security and development governance system. China is willing to work with international community to constructively participate in the second session of the preparatory committee, advance the three pillars of nuclear disarmament, nuclear non-proliferation and the peaceful uses of nuclear energy in a balanced manner, and give full play to the role of the NPT in serving security and development in the new era.

entries.position_summary: The country criticizes Japan’s ocean discharge of treated Fukushima wastewater, warning of bioaccumulation risks and long-term harm to the marine environment, food safety, and human health. It argues Japan has not provided effective assurances or measures to meet its commitments and questions why the water is not managed domestically if it is truly safe.
entries.quote: It may also form a bio-concentration effect, and pose potential risks to food safety and human health, through the migration of marine organisms and the food chain. In the absence of effective measures to ensure that Japan will fulfill its commitments, it is even more difficult to rule out the long-term impact of the ocean discharge on the marine environment and human health. If the so-called "treated-water" is really safe and harmless, why does Japan not dispose of it within its own territory or use it for industrial and agricultural purposes?

entries.position_summary: The country urges Japan to halt its plan to discharge nuclear-contaminated water, insisting it be handled in a science-based, safe, and transparent manner with rigorous international oversight and full consultation with affected stakeholders. It frames this as necessary to protect human health and the environment and to meet international obligations.
entries.quote: 9. In order to protect the only planet on which humanity depends and human life and health, Japan should fully respond to the concerns of China and the international community, and dispose of the nuclear contaminated water in a responsible manner in line with its obligations under international law, stop pushing through the discharge plan, fully consulted with stakeholders including neighbouring countries, make sure to handle the nuclear contaminated water in a science-based, safe and transparent way, and subject itself to rigorous international oversight. 3/3

entries.position_summary: China distinguishes between accident-related 'nuclear contaminated water' and regulated wastewater from normal nuclear plant operations, opposing the former’s discharge into the sea while accepting the latter. This frames its concern as a nuclear safety and environmental protection issue without opposing peaceful nuclear power operations.
entries.quote: What China opposes is the discharge of nuclear contaminated water into the sea, never the discharge of wastewater from the normal operation of nuclear power plants.
